differential_diagnoses: Common Variable Immunodeficiency (CVID) with associated enteropathy
Giardiasis in the setting of hypogammaglobulinemia
next_steps: Surgical consultation for possible small bowel resection due to aneurysmal dilation and suspected ischemia
Repeat cross-sectional imaging or angiography if concern for active hemorrhage persists
Full-thickness tissue sampling/resection of the involved jejunal segments for histopathology and flow cytometry
Assessment of T-cell receptor gene rearrangement studies on biopsy tissue to evaluate for occult lymphoma
Evaluation for secondary causes of immunodeficiency (e.g., genetic panel for CVID-related mutations)
Optimization of nutritional status and iron replacement

differential_diagnoses: Indolent T-cell lymphoproliferative disorder of the gastrointestinal tract
Common Variable Immunodeficiency (CVID) with malabsorption and chronic giardiasis
T-cell large granular lymphocyte (LGL) leukemia with GI involvement
Crohn's disease (refractory, potentially masqueraded by occult lymphoproliferative disorder)
next_steps: Surgical consultation for resection of the jejunal segments demonstrating aneurysmal dilation and suspected ischemia
Full-thickness histopathological evaluation of the resected bowel with immunohistochemistry for T-cell/NK-cell markers (CD3, CD4, CD8, CD56, CD30, EBER)
T-cell receptor (TCR) gene rearrangement studies on the resected tissue to establish clonality
Bone marrow aspiration and biopsy to investigate the peripheral NK-cell population and potential systemic involvement
Flow cytometry of the resected tissue and peripheral blood for precise immunophenotyping of the clonal lymphoid population
Consultation with hematology-oncology for management of potential lymphoproliferative disorder
Continued optimization of nutritional status and supportive care (e.g., iron replacement, vitamin supplementation)
